verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (HSP70-1/Hsp72) is canonically a cytosolic molecular chaperone with no TM domain or GPI anchor. However, it is extensively documented to translocate to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane in tumor cells and stress-exposed cells via direct insertion into lipid bilayers enriched in saturated phosphatidylserine and Gb3-containing lipid rafts. The C-terminal domain is exposed extracellularly in viable tumor cells (confirmed by cmHsp70.1 mAb flow cytometry and surface biotinylation across many tumor lines). This surface pool is absent or negligible in normal unstressed cells, qualifying as cancer/stress-state-induced ecto-display.
